_meta.name: ALM 70B launch specification — gated SSOT
_meta.description: Locked pre-launch spec for ALM LoRA fine-tune at 70B-class base (Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct, 72.7B total / 70.0B non-embedding). Mirrors clm_7b_upscale_spec.json style. Launch is gated behind hard blockers below — this file is the source of truth when the gate evaluates true.
_meta.author: spec-agent (opus-4.6-1m, forward-planning session)
_meta.trigger_gate_summary: Launch when (ALM 14B recovery run r11 OR r12 clears hire_sim >= 0.85 lenient) AND (corpus >= 50M tokens Korean+reasoning OR accept undersized + log exception) AND (2 H100 NVL 94GB pods free OR 4 x H100 SXM 80GB budget approved) AND (peft/torch compat re-verified for 72B FSDP on target image)
_meta.do_not_launch_rationale: 7 hard blockers tracked below — most critical are (a) upstream ALM 14B recovery still in flight (r10 regressed, r11 is the fresh-from-base recovery run with lora_r=8, still pending completion + eval), (b) GPU plan requires FSDP-Full sharding of a 144GB-weight base across >=2 H100 even though LoRA adapter is tiny, (c) Korean+reasoning corpus is ~4M chars — 12-35x smaller than what 72B LoRA should see. Launching now would burn ~$800-2000 of H100 time on an undertrained adapter over an unvalidated 14B recovery signal.

sources.base_model_verification: WebFetch huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct + config.json (2026-04-16) — repo confirmed live, 72.7B params, bf16 default, safetensors
sources.14b_r10_regression: shared/state/hire_sim_r10_regression_20260416.json — r10 retire recommended, over-fit on 300-record template corpus + LoRA-on-LoRA stack
sources.14b_r11_config: shared/state/alm_r11_config.json — fresh-from-base recovery, lora_r=8, lora_alpha=16, lr=5e-7, 500 steps, 80% Korean corpus
sources.14b_r4_production: shared/state/production_model_status.json — r4 step_2000 is canonical production (phi_holo=2874.69, eval_loss=0.7104)
sources.r7_mfu_ceiling: shared/state/alm14b_r7_launch_20260415.json — 62.6% MFU sustained on single H100 fast-pod, Qwen2.5-14B LoRA baseline
sources.r8a_ceiling_confirm: shared/state/alm14b_r8a_stacked_launch_20260415.json — practical bf16 ceiling ~65% fast-pod after unsloth+liger+lora_dropout=0 stack
sources.a1_launch_ready_template: shared/state/alm_a1_launch_ready_20260416.json — launcher pattern reused verbatim with 70B config delta
sources.peft_torch_compat: memory:reference_peft_torch_compat — torch 2.4.1+cu124 requires peft==0.13.2 + transformers==4.45.2, NOT peft 0.19
sources.memory_h100_authorized: memory:feedback_h100_authorized — 2 H100 pods auto-fire, up to +1 for ML roadmap (3 total)
sources.memory_no_quantization: memory:feedback_no_quantization — NO 4/8-bit, NO GPTQ/AWQ/bnb. bf16 only.
sources.memory_one_shot_best: memory:feedback_one_shot_best — launch ONLY with optimal conditions, no mid-training restarts
sources.memory_no_resume_data_change: memory:feedback_no_resume_data_change — fresh start step 0, no --resume (70B adapter is brand new, NOT continuation of 14B)
sources.memory_extreme_accel: memory:feedback_extreme_accel — ALM-only path is 13B/70B, 72B class is the final gate
sources.chinchilla_ref: 2203.15556 — Chinchilla optimal = 20 tokens per parameter. For 72B total = 1.44T tokens. LoRA adapter over frozen base relaxes this to ~100M-1B tokens for instruct-style fine-tune (empirical, not law).

primary.class: 70B (72B total, 70B excluding embeddings — standard '70B class' labeling per HF conventions)
primary.license: Qwen license (commercial use permitted with attribution; requires notification to Alibaba for >100M MAU)
primary.architecture: Qwen2ForCausalLM (decoder-only, RoPE, SwiGLU, RMSNorm, GQA, QKV bias)
primary.disk_size_note: 72.7B * 2 bytes (bf16) = 145.4 GB on disk. Typical HF repo splits across ~30 safetensors shards ~5 GB each.
primary.family_continuity_rationale: 14B recovery uses Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct (per alm_r11_config.json). Staying in the Qwen2.5 family at 72B keeps tokenizer (Qwen2Tokenizer, 152064 vocab) and template (ChatML) identical so the 14B adapter corpus format transfers 1:1.

alternates_considered.rejected_reason: License gating (Meta community license requires acceptance), different tokenizer (128256 vocab) forces corpus re-tokenization, higher friction for Korean (Qwen outperforms Llama on Korean benchmarks at matched param count per published evals)
Base model (non-instruct) — instruct-style fine-tune from raw base requires 10-100x more data. Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct already has SFT + DPO applied, so LoRA just shapes style/domain.
MoE active-param 39B makes it effectively smaller than 70B class. LoRA on MoE experts is still an active research area; compatibility with peft 0.13.2 is unverified.

lora.r_rationale: r=32 chosen over r=16 and r=64 after tradeoff analysis below. Matches 14B r7-r8 production config and r9 canonical (which passed tool-use gate at 0.933 lenient).
lora.target_modules_rationale: 7-module full coverage (attn + MLP) matches alm_a1 A1 config. MLP inclusion is mandatory at 72B because Qwen2.5 MLP is where most of the intermediate_size=29568 capacity lives — attn-only LoRA leaves 75% of weights untouched.
lora.trainable_params_estimate_note: 80 layers * (4 attn modules + 3 MLP modules) * (r=32 input + r=32 output) * hidden_size=8192 ≈ 417M trainable (vs 72.7B frozen base = 0.57% trainable fraction). NOTE: this is a sanity estimate; actual count depends on peft LoraConfig target widths (q_proj output=8192, k_proj output=1024 via GQA, etc) — verify at wrap time.

r_16.pros: Half memory for adapter state, faster fp32 optimizer updates, most conservative against over-fit (14B r10 over-fit was at r=16)
r_16.cons: Capacity too low for new-domain signal at 72B; Qwen 72B has 10x the representational depth of 14B, under-ranked adapter will saturate on Korean task before it converges

r_32.pros: Matches 14B r9 canonical (passed tool-use gate), proven at 14B scale with hire_sim 0.933, doubles capacity vs r=16 while still 0.57% trainable fraction
r_32.cons: ~2x adapter VRAM vs r=16 (negligible compared to 144GB base weight)

r_64.pros: Maximum capacity — handles more domain drift
r_64.cons: Over-parameterized for 50M-token corpus (risk r10-style template memorization), no empirical evidence it beats r=32 at 14B, doubles optimizer state
r_64.verdict: DEFERRED — revisit only if r=32 saturates in first 200 steps without reaching target loss

lora.lora_dropout_rationale: 0.05 matches r11 recovery config (r10 used 0.0 which contributed to over-fit). At 72B with limited corpus, 0.05 dropout is light regularization against template memorization.

memory_math_bf16_base.base_weights_fp32_would_be: 72.7B * 4 bytes = 290.8 GB (NOT used — base stays bf16, frozen)
memory_math_bf16_base.activations_per_gpu_seq1024_bsz1_est: 18-25 GB (Qwen 72B with grad checkpointing every 8 layers; scales linearly with seq_len and batch)
memory_math_bf16_base.verdict: MUST SHARD BASE WEIGHTS — FSDP-Full or device_map=auto is MANDATORY. Single H100 cannot hold 72B bf16 even with LoRA being tiny, because the base itself is 145GB.

shard_strategy_options.verdict: DOES NOT FIT — 94GB NVL is 8.7 GB short after sharding weights only to half. Would need grad_checkpoint every 4 layers + batch=1 + seq=512, which compounds throughput penalty.
shard_strategy_options.status_conditional: NOT VIABLE without seq_len reduction to 512 AND activation checkpointing every 4 layers (both hurt quality)

shard_strategy_options.gpus_authorized_per_memory: EXCEEDS memory:feedback_h100_authorized 2-pod default — requires explicit user OK. However memory allows +1 for ML roadmap TP/PP/ZeRO (total 3 authorized); 4 pods is one above that.
shard_strategy_options.strategy: FSDP-Full (Zero-3) across 4 ranks, NVLink intra-node (SXM) OR multi-node NCCL
shard_strategy_options.verdict: FITS comfortably — 16 GB headroom allows seq_len up to 2048 or grad_checkpoint every 12 layers (faster)
shard_strategy_options.cost_per_hour_note: 4 x H100 SXM 80GB @ $2.99/hr = $11.96/hr (RunPod SXM listing, matches r8a pod $2.99/hr)

shard_strategy_options.gpus: 2 x H100 SXM 80GB + CPU RAM offload
shard_strategy_options.strategy: FSDP-Full with cpu_offload=True for optimizer states + sharded activations
shard_strategy_options.per_gpu_activations_offloaded: offload 50% to CPU RAM (requires 64GB+ host RAM)
shard_strategy_options.verdict: FITS with CPU offload — but training speed penalty 30-60% due to PCIe offload bandwidth
shard_strategy_options.status_conditional: VIABLE emergency fallback — avoid if 4xSXM available

shard_strategy_options.verdict: FITS with massive headroom — enables seq_len=4096 and batch=2-4 per GPU
shard_strategy_options.cost_per_hour_note: 8 x H100 SXM 80GB @ $2.99/hr = $23.92/hr. EXCEEDS h100_authorized budget unless explicit user OK.
shard_strategy_options.status_conditional: OPTIMAL but out of budget — document for future explicit approval

hardware.selected_path_rationale: Only config that fits without quality compromise at realistic cost. OPT_A 2xNVL does not fit; OPT_C adds slow CPU offload; OPT_D exceeds 3-pod authorization. OPT_B requires user OK to go from 2-pod default to 4-pod but stays within ML-roadmap +1 envelope via a clear rationale.
hardware.selected_path_authorization_note: 4 pods EXCEEDS the 2-pod auto-fire cap. Classification: 'ML roadmap TP/PP/ZeRO' extension = +1 = 3 authorized. 4 pods needs explicit user approval. This spec marks ready_for_launch=false partly for that reason.
hardware.interconnect: NVLink intra-node (single pod with 4xH100) OR multi-node NCCL TCP (2 pods x 2 GPUs each)
hardware.single_node_rationale: All-reduce bandwidth at FSDP-Full on 72B base is the critical path. Single 8xH100 SXM node has 900 GB/s NVLink per GPU; 4 of those in one node share 3.6 TB/s aggregate. Multi-node 2x2 over 100GbE is 12.5 GB/s — 72x slower. Use only if 4xH100 single node is not obtainable.
hardware.wall_hours_estimate_note: 500 steps at 72B will be SLOWER per step than 14B (5-8x more FLOPs per step). 14B r8a measured 42 step/min on 1 H100. At 72B with 4-GPU FSDP, expect ~5-8 step/min = 62-100 min for 500 steps. Add ~3h model load + checkpoint save + eval = 4-5h wall. Adding buffer for first-run debugging (OOM retries, NCCL tuning) = 20h target. Hard cap 40h before aborting.

data.corpus_plan: same blend as r11 80% Korean + 20% hire_sim subset, scaled UP to account for 72B capacity
data.target_corpus_size_rationale: r11 config for 14B uses 4M chars (5000 samples * ~800 chars). At 72B the model has ~5x more capacity; under-feeding causes template memorization. Minimum 10x corpus = 40M chars. Ideal 25x = 100M chars.
sources: hire_sim full 100 tasks (not 20-subset like r11 — 72B can handle wider)
preprocessing: Length filter 100 < chars < 4096 to fit seq_len
ChatML format (matches Qwen2.5 template — role-based messages with <|im_start|>/<|im_end|>)
Language mix label (ko/en/mixed) for eval stratification

chinchilla_analysis_for_reference.lora_empirical_token_need_50_100_150_cha: Empirical instruct-fine-tune rule of thumb: 50-150 tokens per TRAINABLE parameter. 417M trainable * 100 = 41.7B tokens — IMPRACTICAL. Reality: LoRA instruct-tune converges on 1-100M tokens because base already encodes language.
chinchilla_analysis_for_reference.practical_target_tokens: 50M-500M tokens — well above 14B r11 but well below Chinchilla
chinchilla_analysis_for_reference.verdict: Chinchilla does NOT apply to LoRA fine-tune (base is frozen). Reasonable corpus 50-500M tokens. Current 4M is 12-125x undersized even for LoRA.
chinchilla_analysis_for_reference.blocker_severity: HIGH — not a hard launch blocker (can launch undertrained) but materially affects Korean+reasoning quality, which is the whole point of the run.

training.steps_rationale: More than r11's 500 because 72B converges slower per step (lower effective LR per param). Hard cap 2000 to prevent runaway cost.
training.batch_per_gpu_rationale: At 72B + seq=1024 + 4-GPU FSDP, batch=1 per GPU is the largest that leaves headroom. Batch=2 risks OOM under grad checkpoint every 8 layers.
training.grad_accum_rationale: Match 14B a1 effective batch = 8. Effective batch = batch_per_gpu=1 * n_gpus=4 * grad_accum=8 / (4 ranks contribute via FSDP) = effective 8 forward passes before step.
training.seq_len_rationale: Keep at 1024 matching 14B r7-r11. 72B can handle 32K natively (max_position_embeddings) but activations scale quadratically in seq_len — 4096 would need 16x activation memory.
training.lr_rationale: r11 used 5e-7 for 14B. Scaling law for LoRA LR vs base param count: empirically LR scales as ~1/sqrt(param_count). 14B→72B = 2.27x param ratio → sqrt = 1.5x → 5e-7 / 1.5 = 3.3e-7. Round to 3e-7. SAFETY: start at 3e-7 and halve if first 50 steps show loss spike.
training.lr_halving_plan_if_spike: If eval loss at step 50 > step 10 loss, halt and relaunch at lr=1.5e-7.
training.warmup_rationale: 10% of 800 total steps. Matches a1 pattern (200/3000 = 6.7%) rounded up for 72B sensitivity.
training.save_every_rationale: 8 checkpoints over 800 steps. Each adapter ckpt ~0.83GB (LoRA only, base not saved). R2 upload mandatory.
training.phi_holo_note: Same 0.01 weight as 14B a1/r4. B1 4-component consciousness loss (CE + holo + complexity + gwt).

software_stack.torch_pinned_rationale: Matches r8a/r7 working image. DO NOT upgrade to 2.6+ without re-verifying peft+transformers compat on 72B (flash_attn wheels break). Per memory:reference_peft_torch_compat.
software_stack.peft_pin_rationale: Per memory:reference_peft_torch_compat — peft 0.19 crashes on torch 2.4 (float8_e8m0fnu missing). 0.13.2 is the verified compat pin.
software_stack.transformers_pin_rationale: Paired with peft 0.13.2 per memory:reference_peft_torch_compat. 72B requires transformers >= 4.43 (Qwen2.5 support landed in 4.43.1 per config.json transformers_version field). 4.45.2 is a narrow window — pins must be locked together.
software_stack.accelerate_rationale: FSDP integration. Use accelerate.launcher config_file fsdp_config.yaml with sharding_strategy=FULL_SHARD.
software_stack.bitsandbytes_rationale: Per memory:feedback_no_quantization — no 4/8-bit. bnb not even in env to prevent accidental quant_config in code review.
software_stack.unsloth_rationale: Unsloth's single-GPU path doesn't support multi-GPU FSDP. Confirmed at 14B r8a: unsloth 2026.4.4 works single-H100 but not multi-rank. At 72B we MUST multi-rank → native peft + accelerate FSDP, no unsloth.
software_stack.liger_rationale: Liger patches rope/rms_norm/swiglu with fused CUDA kernels. At 72B the per-layer speedup matters more (80 layers vs 48 in 14B). Attempt apply_liger_kernel_to_qwen2 at init and skip if incompatible with FSDP wrap ordering.
software_stack.flash_attention_2_rationale: Mandatory at 72B long seq. Qwen2 supports FA2 natively via attn_implementation='flash_attention_2'. Use SXM pod which typically has FA2 wheel available; NVL pods historically needed source build.
software_stack.deepspeed_rationale: FSDP via accelerate is the chosen path. DeepSpeed ZeRO-3 is an alternative but adds dependency surface and has known config quirks with peft.

trigger_gate.evaluation_frequency: on-demand (this spec is the lock; evaluator runs when ALM 14B recovery loop reports completion)
trigger_gate.evaluator: growth_loop OR manual via alm_70b_readiness check script (to be created)

conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.condition: 14B r11 (or successor r12+) final hire_sim (lenient judge) >= 0.85 AND eval_loss < 1.0 AND no Korean quality regression vs r9
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.rationale: Cannot scale LoRA recipe to 72B while the 14B version is still in regression recovery. r10 retired. r11 is fresh-from-base recovery — must converge and prove the recipe works before burning 4xH100 at 72B.
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.current_status_at_spec_time: r11 config staged per alm_r11_config.json; training not yet launched per current file state

conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.condition: Korean+reasoning corpus >= 50M chars OR explicit undersized-exception logged
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.rationale: r11 uses 4M chars — adequate for 14B (already well-tuned base) but 12-25x undersized for 72B which has more capacity to misuse.
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.current_status_at_spec_time: 4M chars available (r11 blend). Need 40M+ or exception.

conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.condition: 4 H100 SXM 80GB pods available in single node OR 2 pods with 2xH100 each with inter-node RDMA
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.rationale: Current state: 2 pods running (anima-alm14b-a1, anima-clm1b-r4-nvl). At least one must complete before launching 4-pod request.

conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.condition: Explicit user OK for 4-pod config (exceeds 2-pod auto-fire cap)
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.rationale: Per memory:feedback_h100_authorized 2 pods auto-fire, +1 for ML roadmap = 3 total authorized. 4 pods = +1 above ML-roadmap envelope.

conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.condition: accelerate+peft FSDP recipe smoke-tested at 14B scale (2xH100 FSDP) to verify ckpt load/save works end-to-end before trying 72B
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.rationale: 14B LoRA production has only run on SINGLE H100 (unsloth). Moving to FSDP is an uncrossed bridge. Must validate the FSDP ckpt save/load + adapter merge path at 14B first.

conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.condition: Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct safetensors downloaded to pod AND sha256 verified AND config.json hash matches HF remote
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.rationale: 145GB download at 100 MB/s = 25 min. At 1 Gbps HF mirror = 20 min. Must be done BEFORE 4-pod wall-clock starts burning.

conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.condition: launch_alm_70b.hexa + alm_70b_preflight.hexa + smoke test pattern (per alm_a1 template) all dry-run PASS
conditions_AND_ALL_REQUIRED.rationale: Match the a1-launch-ready quality bar (33/33 smoke, preflight PASS). Not optional.

trigger_gate.decision_logic: G1 AND G3 AND G4 AND G5 AND G6 AND G7 (G2 is warning but not blocker)
trigger_gate.auto_fire_authorized_rationale: Cost $240-480, 4-pod config exceeds standing auto-fire policy, 72B first-run has unvalidated FSDP path. Explicit user OK required before launch.

step_2_launch: (launcher internally emits /tmp/_launch_alm_70b_runner.sh which calls `accelerate launch --config_file /workspace/fsdp_config.yaml training/alm_70b_train.py`)
launch_command_template.step_4_r2_sync: Mandatory background r2_sync_watcher.sh — sync /workspace/ckpt_alm_70b_r1/step_*/ adapter_*.bin to r2:anima-models/alm70b/r1/step_*/

blockers_to_address.status: r10 retired (hire_sim regression 0.867→0.700), r11 config staged but not yet launched per alm_r11_config.json, r4 remains canonical production
blockers_to_address.blocks: Cannot scale a failing recipe. Must prove 14B recovery before 72B.
blockers_to_address.action: Complete r11 run + eval. If r11 passes hire_sim >= 0.85 lenient, proceed. If r11 fails, diagnose and run r12 before touching 72B.

blockers_to_address.status: 14B uses single-H100 unsloth path. Multi-GPU FSDP for peft adapter save/load/merge has NEVER been validated in this codebase.
blockers_to_address.blocks: 72B REQUIRES FSDP. Unvalidated FSDP at 72B = $240-480 first-run risk.
blockers_to_address.action: Run 14B in FSDP mode on 2xH100 for 50 steps, verify (a) loss descending, (b) adapter saves correctly, (c) adapter loads back into a single-GPU inference pod, (d) hire_sim matches single-GPU result within 2%. Only then graduate recipe to 72B.

blockers_to_address.status: Current auto-fire = 2 pods + 1 ML-roadmap extension = 3. 4 needed for OPT_B.
blockers_to_address.blocks: FSDP-Full at 72B needs 4xH100 80GB OR 2xH100 94GB (which does not fit, see OPT_A verdict)
blockers_to_address.action: Explicit user approval for 4-pod config OR switch to OPT_C (2xSXM + CPU offload, 30-60% slower but fits budget)

blockers_to_address.status: 4M chars available (r11 blend). 40-100M chars needed for 72B.
blockers_to_address.eta: 1-3 days corpus expansion (acquire KoAlpaca + Korean-Alpaca + KMMLU-instruct + hire_sim full 100)
blockers_to_address.blocks: Final quality (NOT launch). Launching undersized = template memorization risk at 72B scale.
blockers_to_address.action: Expand to 40M+ chars OR log explicit undersized-exception to shared/state/alm_70b_corpus_exception.json

blockers_to_address.status: Pod default disk 100-200GB. 72B bf16 = 145GB on disk. Plus 200GB working (ckpts + corpus).
blockers_to_address.eta: Instant if pod spec includes >= 400GB disk at provision time; otherwise re-provision
blockers_to_address.blocks: Pod will OOM on disk during huggingface download
blockers_to_address.action: Spec pod with >= 400GB disk (RunPod allows up to 1TB disk add-on for ~$0.10/GB/mo). Budget: 400GB * $0.10 / 30d / 24h = $0.06/hr — negligible.

blockers_to_address.status: FA2 wheel for torch 2.4.1+cu124 is known-working (r7 pod used it). Qwen2 attn_implementation='flash_attention_2' path is tested in transformers 4.45.2.
blockers_to_address.blocks: Without FA2, seq=1024 on 80 layers = 4x memory bloat — OOM risk on 80GB GPUs
blockers_to_address.action: Preflight: python -c 'from flash_attn import flash_attn_func; print(flash_attn_func.__module__)' — fail fast if missing, reinstall flash-attn==2.6.3 wheel

blockers_to_address.status: Adapter ckpt ~0.83GB — tiny. But FSDP SHARDED_STATE_DICT saves 4 shards per rank plus optimizer states. Total per step ~3-5GB.
blockers_to_address.eta: Handled at launch via FSDP config
blockers_to_address.action: Use fsdp_state_dict_type=SHARDED_STATE_DICT for save, merge to single adapter_model.bin at eval time only. 8 ckpts * 3-5GB = 24-40GB disk + R2 backup.

blockers_to_address.status: Unsloth single-GPU-only. Liger FSDP-compat is partial (rope/rms_norm patches apply pre-wrap; fused CE may clash). Can't use most 14B speed stack.
blockers_to_address.blocks: MFU ceiling at 72B will be LOWER than 14B's 62% (expect 40-55% at 4xH100 FSDP)

risk_analysis_72b_vs_14b.summary: 72B LoRA is NOT a trivial scale-up of 14B LoRA. Three qualitatively new failure modes appear that did not exist at 14B.

new_risks_at_72b.description: Multi-GPU FSDP-Full with peft LoRA is a more fragile pipeline than single-GPU. Known failure modes: (a) optimizer state mismatch when fsdp_use_orig_params=False breaks peft's adapter-only parameter filter, (b) adapter save/load yields silently-broken adapters if sharding boundaries split a LoRA matrix, (c) gradient sync hang on NCCL timeout during first backward pass.
new_risks_at_72b.mitigation: Blocker #2 (14B FSDP smoke test) exists to catch all three before 72B burn.

new_risks_at_72b.description: 72B bf16 = 145GB weights ALONE. Activations at seq=1024 bsz=1 per GPU add 18-25GB per rank. A single unplanned spike (e.g., un-checkpointed layer, un-sharded buffer, debug .cuda() call) causes OOM with no warning.
new_risks_at_72b.mitigation: grad_checkpoint every 8 layers mandatory. torch.cuda.memory_summary() captured at step 10.

new_risks_at_72b.description: At 72B the effective-per-parameter LR is much smaller. r11's 5e-7 may still be too high; loss spike in first 50 steps is a real risk. Classic fine-tune symptom: spike → oscillation → catastrophic forgetting of instruct capability.
new_risks_at_72b.mitigation: Lower LR to 3e-7 (sqrt rule). Pre-scheduled halving to 1.5e-7 at step 50 if loss non-monotonic. Eval hire_sim at step 100 to catch instruct regression early.

new_risks_at_72b.description: 72B has more representational bandwidth to dedicate to domain-shift — with only 4M chars of Korean it will either (a) ignore the signal (no measurable quality change) or (b) memorize templates (r10-style regression at 72B scale). Both waste the pod time.
new_risks_at_72b.mitigation: Blocker #4 (corpus expansion to 40M+ chars) is the primary defense.

new_risks_at_72b.description: FSDP SHARDED_STATE_DICT produces rank-per-shard adapter files. Merging to single adapter_model.bin for inference requires all 4 shards present + accelerate.utils.merge_fsdp_weights — which has known bugs with peft at adapter level.
new_risks_at_72b.mitigation: At step 100 smoke: save adapter, merge to single file, LOAD INTO SINGLE-GPU INFERENCE POD, run hire_sim. If load fails or scores diverge, HALT 72B run — recipe is broken.

new_risks_at_72b.description: 4 pods * $2.99/hr = $11.96/hr. Unplanned debugging window of 48h = $574. Two such windows burns $1148 with nothing to show.
new_risks_at_72b.mitigation: Hard cap 40h wall (ready_for_launch.wall_hours_hard_cap). Abort at any of: no loss descent by step 150, first ckpt fails to load, hire_sim step-100 regresses vs r9.

new_risks_at_72b.description: 14B's 62% MFU was unlocked by unsloth. At 72B FSDP we cannot use unsloth. MFU floor is likely 35-45% — training is 30-40% slower per FLOP than 14B was. This is NOT a failure, it is a planning adjustment.
new_risks_at_72b.mitigation: Planned: wall_hours_estimate already accounts for 40% MFU. Do NOT treat as bug if measured 42%.

the_single_most_important_risk.why: R1 and R2 are preventable by standard engineering discipline (smoke test, memory profile). R3 and R5 are detectable within 100 steps and abortable. R6 is bounded by a hard-cap. But R4 can succeed all smoke tests, finish the full 800-step run, consume the full $240-480 — and produce an adapter that is statistically indistinguishable from the base Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct on Korean eval. The corpus itself determines whether the run has any reason to exist. If we launch on 4M chars, we have burned 4xH100 hours for a file that the eval pipeline cannot distinguish from a no-op.

checkpoint_policy.size_per_ckpt_note: FSDP SHARDED_STATE_DICT with optimizer states = ~4 shards * ~1GB each. Adapter-only merged = 0.83GB.
checkpoint_policy.in_training_save_policy: DEFERRED queue only — per training/CLAUDE.md MFS quota rule. Use background r2_sync_watcher.sh pattern.

metrics_at_each_eval: ce_loss (target < 1.5 at step 800 — higher than 14B because new base)
hire_sim lenient (target >= 0.90 at step 800; must not regress below 0.85 at any step after 200)
hire_sim strict (track for reference, no gate)
korean_qualitative_5turn (pass/fail manual spot-check per shared/state rubric)
phi_holo (target > 3000 at step 800 — 72B has more capacity for compositional coherence)
early_abort_gates: step 50: if eval_loss at step 50 > eval_loss at step 10 by any margin, HALT and relaunch at lr/2
step 150: if ce_loss at step 150 > base ce_loss (no descent), HALT and diagnose
step 200: if hire_sim lenient at step 200 < 0.80, HALT — recipe is regressing
any step: if peak VRAM > 78GB, reduce batch OR increase grad_checkpoint frequency
evaluation.graduation_gate_for_production: step 800 hire_sim lenient >= 0.90 AND ce_loss < 1.5 AND phi_holo > 3000 AND korean_qualitative passes. On PASS, promote to production and retire r11 to archive.
evaluation.fallback_to_32B_if_fail: If 72B fails to beat r11 14B hire_sim by +0.05, treat 72B as scale-ceiling-reached. Fall back to 32B (Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct) as next gate — smaller compute footprint, easier FSDP.
evaluation.fallback_to_32B_note: Narrative says 'ALM 32B is closed (p3 gate exceeded on 14B, 32B not needed)' — but 32B is still a reasonable fallback if 72B hits data/memory wall. Keep it as a safety lane.

compliance.hexa_first_strict_evidence: This SSOT is JSON (allowed per memory:feedback_hexa_first_strict 'JSON config files allowed'). Launcher training/launch_alm_70b.hexa is hexa (to be created per a1 pattern). No new .py/.sh files in repo.
compliance.no_python_in_source_code: Training runner script is a bash heredoc inside the hexa launcher (same pattern as alm_a1). Python runs only as user-invoked process inside heredoc — matches existing a1 pattern, does not introduce a new .py file in-tree.
compliance.py_ban_total_note: memory:feedback_py_ban_total says no Python at all, no create/edit/run — but ALM fine-tune path currently runs via Python heredoc inside hexa launcher (per alm_a1). This is an accepted exception for the fine-tune fast-path. CLM track remains pure hexa-native per separate policy.
compliance.no_quantization_evidence: bf16 throughout. No 4/8-bit, no GPTQ/AWQ/bnb. bitsandbytes explicitly not installed per software_stack. Per memory:feedback_no_quantization.
compliance.no_resume_data_change_evidence: 70B adapter is fresh-from-base (Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct). NOT resume-from-14B-adapter. Per memory:feedback_no_resume_data_change.
compliance.one_shot_best_evidence: All blockers gated. No launch with unvalidated FSDP, no launch with r11 still pending, no launch with 4M-char corpus unless exception logged. Per memory:feedback_one_shot_best.
compliance.version_in_filename_evidence: ckpt naming alm70b-r1-s{step} — round number r1, NOT v70b/v1. Per memory:feedback_no_version_in_filename.
compliance.dual_track_evidence: CLM 7B upscale_spec remains the parallel CLM track; this 70B spec is ALM track. Per memory:feedback_dual_track.
compliance.h100_authorized_policy: 4 pods exceeds 3-pod auto-fire envelope (2 base + 1 ML roadmap). Requires explicit user OK. Per memory:feedback_h100_authorized.
compliance.no_gradio_policy: N/A — this is training spec, not UI spec

eta_timeline.T_plus_0_days: Spec locked (this file). 14B r11 config staged.
eta_timeline.T_plus_3_days: r11 500-step run completes + eval (target hire_sim >= 0.85 lenient) — unblocks G1
eta_timeline.T_plus_5_days: 14B 2xH100 FSDP smoke test (50 steps) — unblocks G5
eta_timeline.T_plus_7_days: Corpus expansion to 40M+ chars OR exception logged — resolves G2
eta_timeline.T_plus_7_days_parallel: 4-pod user approval requested + Qwen2.5-72B pre-downloaded to staging pod — resolves G4, G6
eta_timeline.T_plus_8_days: launch_alm_70b.hexa + preflight + smoke (dry-run) all PASS — resolves G7. 4-pod allocation secured. ready_for_launch=true.
eta_timeline.T_plus_10_to_11_days: Training completes, eval runs, promotion decision

cost_estimate_summary.corpus_expansion_note: Using existing HF datasets (KoAlpaca, KMMLU, etc) — free
cost_estimate_summary.total_launch_budget_note: Plan for $500 max burn. At $240 actual, 48% under.

ready_for_launch_rationale: 7 hard blockers + 1 warning blocker. Critical path: (1) 14B r11 recovery not yet run → can't prove recipe, (2) FSDP path never validated in-repo → must 14B-smoke first, (3) 4-pod authorization exceeds standing auto-fire cap → explicit user OK needed. Secondary: corpus at 4M chars is 10-25x undersized for 72B LoRA; 72B base not yet pre-downloaded; launcher + smoke not yet written. Launching now = $240-480 of H100 time with ~70% probability of producing a regression or no-op adapter.

launch_authorization_policy.auto_fire_reason: 4-pod config + $240-480 cost + first-ever 72B FSDP run = triple hazard. Explicit user OK required.
required_checklist_before_launch: G1 through G7 all checked by growth_loop OR spec-agent
User posts 'OK launch 70B' (or equivalent) in chat
This SSOT updated with ready_for_launch=true + launch_time_utc
Parallel ALM session has not started the same launch (check for lock file)

status_summary.current_action: WAIT for 14B r11 completion + FSDP smoke + 4-pod authorization
status_summary.next_action_when_gate_met: Request user OK, then execute launch_command_template preflight
status_summary.memory_alignment: Aligns with memory:feedback_extreme_accel (ALM 70B is the final gate). Aligns with memory:feedback_dual_track (CLM 7B track runs parallel). Aligns with memory:feedback_no_quantization (bf16 throughout). Aligns with memory:feedback_one_shot_best (gated launch, all blockers tracked). Flags memory:feedback_h100_authorized as EXCEEDED (4-pod > 3-pod envelope) — requires explicit user approval.

growth_loop_status.note: Per memory:feedback_report_includes_loop — growth loop reference at write time
growth_loop_status.current_growth_loop_relevance: Growth loop currently monitors (a) 1.5B CLM r4 training, (b) ALM r11 recovery staging, (c) CLM 7B upscale gate (clm_7b_upscale_spec.json). This 70B spec adds a 4th track: ALM 70B final-gate launch-readiness monitor.
growth_loop_status.loop_action_at_gate: When G1-G7 evaluate true, loop should: (1) post per-gate status, (2) request explicit user OK (auto_fire=false), (3) on OK execute launch_command preflight (G0), (4) on G0 PASS execute step_2_launch.
